name: Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra
description: This moderate 20.6-mile mountain biking route from Elche de la Sierra follows the Segura River through canyons and past scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Elche De La Sierra features diverse and rugged landscapes within the Sierra de Segura region. The area is characterized by mountainous terrain, including hills and valleys, with significant elevation changes. The Segura River flows through the region, forming impressive canyons and offering routes alongside its course. This varied topography provides a range of off-road cycling opportunities. Best mountain bike trails around Elche De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Segura River through its canyons. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra, a difficult 31.3 miles (50.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to the notable Letur Waterfall. Local mountain bikers also love the Molinicos – Molinicos loop from Molinicos, a 22.9 miles (36.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Elche De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, river canyons, and rugged hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Elche De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Elche De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Elche De La Sierra?
A: You'll find over 20 mountain bike trails around Elche De La Sierra, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region features 13 moderate routes and 7 more challenging, difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Elche De La Sierra?
A: The terrain around Elche De La Sierra is diverse and rugged, characterized by mountainous landscapes within the Sierra de Segura. Riders can expect a mix of hills, valleys, and impressive canyons carved by the Segura River. Many routes feature significant elevation changes, with some climbs reaching up to 1,000 meters (3,000 feet), providing both challenging ascents and exhilarating descents.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Elche De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Elche De La Sierra are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176794272/sendas-del-rio-segura-circular-desde-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.1 km loop, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176823702/letur-waterfall-circular-from-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.3 km circular path.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners in Elche De La Sierra?
A: While there are no trails specifically rated as 'easy' in our current selection, the region offers 13 moderate routes that can be suitable for riders with some experience or families looking for a less strenuous adventure. These trails often follow the river or offer less extreme elevation changes compared to the difficult routes.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools in Elche De La Sierra?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its beautiful water features. The challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176823702/letur-waterfall-circular-from-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; route leads directly to the notable Letur Waterfall. You can also find natural pools like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de Las Canales&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5639374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Natural Pools of Arroyo Rambla de Benízar&lt;/a&gt;, which are great spots to visit along or near some routes.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks to see while mountain biking?
A: The trails around Elche De La Sierra offer stunning views and access to interesting landmarks. You can ride to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amílcar Barca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador La Rodea Grande&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fuensanta Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The San Blas Outcrop, at 871 meters, is also a prominent natural sight nearby.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Segura River?
A: Yes, the Segura River is a central feature of the region's mountain biking. The popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176794272/sendas-del-rio-segura-circular-desde-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; route follows the river through its impressive canyons, offering picturesque views and a moderate riding experience. The river also provides bathing areas like La Longuera and the Andrés Ravine.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Elche De La Sierra?
A: Elche De La Sierra is generally suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are milder, but it's always wise to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Elche De La Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse and rugged landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the Segura River and its canyons. The variety of trails, from moderate to difficult, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any other towns or historical sites I can visit while mountain biking?
A: Yes, the region offers several charming towns and historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Letur&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the settlements of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible via or near mountain bike routes. These offer a glimpse into the local culture and history.

Q: Are there any trails that might require pushing my bike?
A: Due to the rugged and technical nature of some of the more difficult trails, particularly those with significant elevation changes or very steep sections, it's possible you might encounter portions where pushing your bike is necessary. This is part of the challenging experience for advanced riders in the region.
tips: Beautiful paths next to the Segura river. Impressive views of the Loma de la Muela, a great canyon.

A natural viewpoint located on top of a cliff, from where you can enjoy impressive views. The name of the viewpoint refers to the Carthaginian general Amílcar Barca, who is believed to have passed through this area on his military campaign through the Iberian Peninsula in the 3rd century BC. It is a very nice picnic area, with details on the tables and chairs and even a ''selfie point''.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sendas del río Segura mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It covers 33.1 kilometers with an elevation gain of 853 meters. Riders should have good fitness and some mountain biking experience, as it aligns with Komoot's 'Moderate' rating, which can involve an STS S2 technical difficulty level.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this route is around 3 hours and 26 minutes. However, depending on your pace and stops, it could take up to 5 hours, which is typical for a moderate Komoot mountain bike trail.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sendas del río Segura route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain mix. You'll encounter approximately 13.0 km of 'senda' (trail), 9.41 km of 'sendero estrecho' (narrow path), 8.70 km of 'carretera' (road), and 1.93 km of 'calle' (street), including a very short national road section. Be prepared for potentially rocky sections, technical steps, and areas with loose sand and stone, similar to other trails in the Sierra de Elche area.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This is a circular trail that starts and ends in Elche de la Sierra. You can typically find parking within the town itself, as many trails in the area depart from the town center.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful natural landscapes, including areas like La Longuera and El Gallego. A significant highlight is the Segura River, which carves impressive canyons. You'll also pass by the Amílcar Barca Viewpoint, offering scenic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, considering the 'arid mountains' and 'crystalline waters' of the Segura River, spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking in this region. The weather is usually milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Is Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specify restrictions for dogs on this particular trail. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to ride this specific trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that follow parts of the GR-68, Variante GR-68, Caminio de los Pradillos, Calle el Mojón, and Puente de la Longuera.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Elche de la Sierra, you will find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants within the town where you can refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, 33.1 km distance, and 853 meters of elevation gain, this trail is generally more suitable for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness rather than young children or beginners. There are likely more family-friendly options in the broader Elche de la Sierra area for less experienced riders.
